Aquila Rift.notes: Major dust complex in Aquila/Serpens at ~476 pc. Part of the Great Rift, the dark lane splitting the Milky Way.

Cygnus Rift.notes: Dense dust cloud in Cygnus at ~754 pc. Part of the Great Rift running through the Milky Way near Deneb.

Scutum Cloud.notes: Dust concentration in Scutum at ~605 pc, marking the near edge of the Scutum-Centaurus spiral arm.

Vela Molecular Ridge.notes: Extensive molecular cloud complex in Vela at ~872 pc. One of the largest nearby star-forming regions.

Ophiuchus-Scorpius Cloud.notes: Nearest dense cloud complex at ~252 pc in Ophiuchus/Scorpius. Contains the Rho Ophiuchi star-forming region.

Cepheus Cloud.notes: Molecular cloud in Cepheus at ~542 pc. Active low-mass star formation region.

Taurus-Perseus Cloud.notes: Extended dust cloud at ~356 pc between Taurus and Perseus. Part of the Gould Belt structure.

Vulpecula Rift.notes: Dark cloud complex in Vulpecula at ~627 pc, part of the extended Aquila Rift in the Local Arm.

Vela Cloud.notes: Front face of the Vela Molecular Ridge at ~750 pc, associated with the Vela OB2 association.

Cygnus X.notes: Southern component of the Cygnus X star-forming complex at ~939 pc, one of the most massive molecular regions within 2 kpc.

Vulpecula OB1 Cloud.notes: Molecular cloud associated with the Vulpecula OB1 stellar association at ~1053 pc in the Local Arm.

Cassiopeia Cloud.notes: Molecular cloud complex in Cassiopeia at ~727 pc, associated with Cas OB2/OB5 on the near side of the Perseus Arm.

Cepheus-Cassiopeia Cloud.notes: Nearby molecular cloud at ~337 pc at the boundary of the Local Bubble, part of the Cepheus-Cassiopeia complex.

Camelopardalis Cloud.notes: Molecular cloud associated with the Cam OB1 association at ~918 pc at the outer edge of the Local Arm.

California Molecular Cloud.notes: Massive giant molecular cloud at ~446 pc rivaling Orion A in size. Discovered relatively recently despite its proximity.

Aquila Rift (far).notes: Distant component of the Aquila Rift dark cloud complex at ~754 pc, toward the Scutum tangent.

Cepheus Flare.notes: Complex of molecular clouds at ~450 pc in Cepheus, site of low-mass star formation.

Taurus Molecular Cloud.notes: One of the nearest star-forming regions at ~184 pc. A classic low-mass star formation laboratory in Taurus-Auriga.

Cepheus OB3 Cloud.notes: Molecular cloud at ~770 pc associated with the Cep OB3 stellar association, an active region of sequential star formation.

Cygnus OB7 Cloud.notes: Dark molecular cloud complex at ~670 pc associated with Cygnus OB7, part of the Great Rift in Cygnus.

Canis Major Cloud.notes: Molecular cloud complex at ~832 pc associated with the CMa OB1 association and CMa R1 reflection nebula.

Lupus Far Cloud.notes: Distant dust concentration at ~1019 pc beyond the Lupus/Upper Sco complex, near the Scutum-Crux arm.
